person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
“Royal Ancestry: A Study in Colonial & Medieval Families,” Douglas Richardson (2013):
  “ROGER DE TOSNY, Knt., of Flamstead, Hertfordshire, and seigneur of Conches, Acquigny, Nogent-le-Roi, Portes, and Toéni (now Tosny), born about 1160 (of age about 1180). 
He married in 1175 CONSTANCE DE BEAUMONT, daughter of Richard de Beaumont, Viscount of Beaurnont-en-Maine, seigneur of Beaumont-le-Vicomte, Fresnay, and Ste.-Suzanne, by Lucy, daughter of Richer de seigneur of by his wife, Béatrix [see BEAUMONT 4 for her ancestry]. 
They had three sons, 
1. Ralph, Knt., 
2. Roger, Knt., and 
3. Richard [Treasurer of Angers], 
and one daughter, 
4. Margaret (wife of Malcolm, 6th Earl of Fife). 
About 1190-1200 he granted the nuns of Litdemore, Oxfordshire 20 acres of his demesne upon Moderul, Oxfordshire. Shortly before his departure on Crusade to Palestine in 1190, he and Gilbert de Tillières attended King Richard I at Verneuil, where the king lent them his seal for a grant which they wished to make to Saint-Evroul. In the same year, Roger likewise issued an act at Nogent-le-Roi for the neighboring Abbey of Coulombs. He was present at Acre in 1191. He was with King Richard I at Speyer, Austria in 1194. In 1196 he served as surety in the treaty concluded between the Count of Flanders and the King of France. In 1199 the king restored the manor of Saham Toney, Norfolk to him. The same year his wife, Constance, was granted the manor of Alrichescote (in South Tawton), Devon by King John. The same year King John ordered that Constance, Lady of Conches, be quit of the debt of 21 silver marks which she owed Benoit the Jew of Verneuil upon the payment of the principal. Roger served as one of the sureties for King John of England for the treaty of Le Goulet in 1200. Following the capitulation of Rouen in 1204, Roger lost his French and Norman lands. He was granted the hundreds of Grimeshou and Weyland, Norfolk by King John. In 1206 a dispute arose between him and the canons of St. Paul's regarding their right of common in a wood in Caddington, Bedfordshire. He founded the nunnery of St Giles in the Wood, near Flamstead, Hertfordshire. At an unknown date, he issued a charter for the lepers of Chartres. 
SIR ROGER DE TONY died in 1208-9. 
His widow, Constance, was living in 1226. She died 2 October, before 1236.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Roger de Tosny於1156年出生在英國賀特福爾福拉姆斯亞德時，他的父親Roger de Tosny IV22歲，母親Baroness Isabel Leicester27歲。他於大約1188年與Constance de Beaumont結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1208年12月29日在英國英格蘭賀特福爾福拉姆斯亞德享年52歲，埋葬在英國賀特福爾福拉姆斯亞德。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Tosny
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Jean Pierre.
Americanized form of any of various surnames ultimately derived from the Latin personal name Antonius (see Anthony ), for example Greek Antoniou and Italian Toni .
French: from the personal name Tony, a pet form of Antonin (from Latin Antoninus, a derivative of Antonius; see Anthony ).

variants.name: Anthony
meanings: English and West Indian (mainly Antigua and Barbuda, Saint Lucia, and Trinidad and Tobago, also Dutch Caribbean): from the personal name Anthony, Latin Antonius, which, with its variants and cognates, is one of the commonest personal names in Europe. Spellings with -h-, which first appear in English in the 16th century and in French (as Anthoine) at about the same time, are due to the erroneous belief that the name derives from Greek anthos ‘flower’. The popularity of the personal name in Christendom is largely due to the cult of the Egyptian hermit Saint Anthony ( AD 251–356), who in his old age gathered a community of hermits around him, and for that reason is regarded by some as the founder of monasticism. It was further increased by the fame of Saint Anthony of Padua (1195–1231), who long enjoyed a great popular cult and who is believed to help people find lost things. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates and derivatives (patronymics) from other languages, for example Greek patronymic Antoniades , Italian Antoni , Polish Antoniewicz , Croatian and Serbian Antonović (see Antonovich ) and Antunović; see also below. The name Anthony is also found among Christians in southern India, but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. Compare Antony .

German, Flemish, and French (mainly Alsace): Latinized (humanistic) patronymic from local equivalents of the Latin personal name Antonius, from its genitive form Antoni(i). In North America, this surname is also an altered form of the German, Dutch, French, and Slovak cognates Antoni 1 and Antony 2.
History: John Anthony of Hampstead, Middlesex, England (now part of north London) migrated to Boston, MA, in 1634. By 1640 he had moved to Providence, RI, where his descendants are still established.

nameMeanings.name: Roger
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name, Rog(i)er, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, from hrōd ‘fame’ + gār, gēr ‘spear’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain, replacing the native Old English form Hrōðgār. Roger was one of the most popular boys' names throughout the medieval period, but less so after the Reformation, though it has continued in regular use to the present day. Roger, Count of Sicily ( c.1031–1101 ), son of Tancred, recovered Sicily from the Arabs. His son, also called Roger, ruled Sicily as king, presiding over a court noted for its splendour and patronage of the arts.

variants.name: Rodger
meanings: Variant spelling of   Roger  , in part from the surname derived from the given name in the Middle Ages.

en.lifeSummary: When Roger de Tosny was born in 1156, in Flamstead, Hertfordshire, England, his father, Roger de Tosny IV, was 22 and his mother, Baroness Isabel Leicester, was 27. He married Constance de Beaumont about 1188. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 29 December 1208, in Flamstead, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 52, and was buried in Flamstead, Hertfordshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Roger de Tosny est né en 1156, à Flamstead, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, son père, Roger de Tosny IV, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Baroness Isabel Leicester, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé Constance de Beaumont environ 1188. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 29 décembre 1208, à Flamstead, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 52 ans, et a été enterré à Flamstead, Hertfordshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Roger de Tosny 1156 in Flamstead, Hertfordshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Roger de Tosny IV 22 und seine Mutter Baroness Isabel Leicester 27. Er hat um 1188 Constance de Beaumont geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 29. Dezember 1208 im Alter von 52 Jahren in Flamstead, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Flamstead, Hertfordshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Roger de Tosny è nato il nel 1156 nel Flamstead, Hertfordshire, England, il suo padre, Roger de Tosny IV, aveva 22 anni e il suo madre, Baroness Isabel Leicester, aveva 27 anni. Lui sposato Constance de Beaumont circa 1188. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 29 dicembre 1208, nel Flamstead, Hertfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 52 anni, e fu sepolto a Flamstead, Hertfordshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Roger de Tosnyが1156年にイギリスハートフォードシュアイアフラムステアドで生まれた時，父Roger de Tosny IVは22歳，母Baroness Isabel Leicesterは27歳であった。約1188年にConstance de Beaumontと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1208年12月29日にイギリスイギリスハートフォードシュアイアフラムステアドで52歳で死去し，イギリスハートフォードシュアイアフラムステアドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Roger de Tosny(이)가 1156년 영국 허트퍼드실르 플램스테아드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Roger de Tosny IV(은)는 22세였고, 그의 어머니 Baroness Isabel Leicester(은)는 27세였다. 그는 대략 1188년 Constance de Beaumont(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1208년 12월 29일 영국 잉글랜드 허트퍼드실르 플램스테아드에서 52세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 허트퍼드실르 플램스테아드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Roger de Tosny nasceu em 1156, em Flamstead, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Roger de Tosny IV, tinha 22 anos e sua mãe, Baroness Isabel Leicester, tinha 27 anos. Ele casou-se com Constance de Beaumont aproximadamente 1188. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 29 de dezembro de 1208, em Flamstead, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 52 anos, e foi sepultado em Flamstead, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Roger de Tosny родился в 1156 г. в местности флэмстид, хиртфордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Roger de Tosny IV было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Baroness Isabel Leicester, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени Constance de Beaumont около 1188 г. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер 29 декабря 1208 г. в местности флэмстид, хиртфордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 52 лет и был похоронен в местности флэмстид, хиртфордшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Roger de Tosny nació en 1156, en Flamstead, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Roger de Tosny IV, tenía 22 años y su mamá, Baroness Isabel Leicester, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Constance de Beaumont aproximadamente 1188. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 29 de diciembre de 1208, en Flamstead, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 52 años, y fue sepultado en Flamstead, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra.
